channel.title: Lme4 on Lisa DeBruine
channel.description: Recent content in Lme4 on Lisa DeBruine

item.title: Simulating Mixed Effects
item.description: [Updated 2019-01-09] I’m going to walk through one example of simulating a dataset with random effects. I’ll generate data for a Stroop task where people (subjects) say the colour of colour words (stimuli) shown in each of two versions (congruent and incongruent). Subjects are in one of two conditions (hard and easy). The dependent variable (DV) is reaction time. congruent: RED, ORANGE, YELLOW, GREEN, BLUE, PURPLE incongruent: RED, ORANGE, YELLOW, GREEN, BLUE, PURPLE We expect people to have faster reaction times for congruent stimuli than incongruent stimuli (main effect of version) and to be faster in the easy condition than the hard condition (main effect of condition).
